sources_audited.note: The cited Census NAICS page is reachable, but its fetched content does not display the 523150 definition or the asserted underwriting, agency-brokerage, and principal-dealing details; it therefore does not verify the registered claim as cited.

The May 2023 BLS NAICS 523000 table reports the cited broader-subsector occupation shares, including 39.70% business and financial operations, 17.33% sales, 19.18% office support, 15.29% management, and 6.45% computer and mathematical occupations.

O*NET lists the cited securities-sales tasks, including market monitoring, client interviews, transaction records and regulatory review, proposals, financial reports, recommendations, pricing, negotiation, and execution.
Census reports 33.9% current AI use in Finance and Insurance as of May 3, 2026 and roughly 39% expected use over the next six months, under the revised any-business-function wording.

Gallup reports a 22% five-year sell-or-transfer intention among employer-business owners and presents the employer summary for sell, transfer, or take public; the packet appropriately identifies this as a broad intention proxy rather than completed deals.
FINRA Rule 1017 requires covered ownership, control, asset, and business-operation changes to be submitted for approval with Form CMA disclosures and expressly contemplates rejection or denial.
BLS projects 3% employment growth for securities, commodities, and financial-services sales agents from 2024 to 2034 and describes their market-intermediation role.

The SEC guide supports the cited broker/dealer definitions, registration obligations, transaction-related compensation indicators, placement-agent coverage, and associated-person supervision requirements.

FINRA Rule 3110 requires supervisory systems, written procedures, appropriately registered principals, and written principal review of investment-banking and securities transactions and communications.

caveats: S1's exact cited page does not expose the registered six-digit definition in fetched content, leaving the lens-heterogeneity claim unverified from that citation; because n is already MISSING, correcting or downgrading this support cannot change the absent base tier or EVIDENCE_FIRST action.

The a estimate is an honest but uncalibrated synthesis of employment shares for broad NAICS 523000 and tasks for one broad securities-sales occupation, not a payroll-weighted task census for the narrowed 523150 population.

The rho range extrapolates from sector-wide use of AI in any business function to the five-year share of exposed labor actually implemented; the source does not measure workflow completion, saved hours, or independent broker-dealer adoption.
The s5 base discounts a cross-industry survey of owner intentions into completed regulated control transfers without observed broker-dealer completion or retention data.

The d5 base translates a ten-year, economy-wide occupational employment projection into five-year constant-price demand for a narrowed service basket; the bridge and error directions are disclosed, but the quantitative mapping remains loose.

The q and o values are explicitly labeled ESTIMATE and supported only by qualitative fee-structure and regulatory evidence; no source measures retained AI benefit or the future operator-required service share.
summary: Mechanics pass and the record is useful and candid about its missing firm-stock anchor, broad proxies, estimate-heavy ranges, and coherent disclosed agency-focused lens. Eight cited sources support their registered underlying claims; S1 is reachable but does not expose the claimed NAICS definition in fetched content. No issue meets the frozen materiality test because n is already MISSING, leaving no base tier and fixing the action at EVIDENCE_FIRST, so the appropriate outcome is publishable_with_caveats.
